core_mechanism: constraint-driven condensation via Rayleigh-Jeans thermalization: a system of coupled nonlinear oscillators with two simultaneously conserved quantities (total energy and total norm/probability) relaxes to the maximum-entropy equilibrium distribution over its modes, forcing a finite fraction of the conserved 'mass' to condense into the lowest-energy states and yielding a heavy-tailed (Pareto/Lorenz-type) distribution of the conserved quantity across components
brief_reason: The abstract maps vote fractions onto energies of coupled nonlinear oscillators whose evolution conserves total energy and probability norm, driving Rayleigh-Jeans thermalization and low-energy condensation that reproduces the Lorenz/Pareto inequality curves.
